title: The Mystery of Gendos Office
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Fri Jul 22, 2005 11:59 am Reichu wrote: She breaks out of the bakelite, makes Shinji hop on into her spine, blows a hole through the pyramid to the surface, levitates and sprouts two wings, and then pops off the pylons and sprouts four in a cross arrangement. Thankfully, continuity didn't screw up on that one, so Yui-sama is pylon-less for the remainder of eternity.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Mon Jul 25, 2005 1:48 pm eh? shit, continuity has failed yet again when exactly do you see the pyramid afterwards?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 25, 2005 2:31 pm You don't.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Jul 25, 2005 3:01 pm Reichu wrote: You don't.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 25, 2005 3:27 pm Kind of funny that the top of the pyramid is where Gendo's office was.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Jul 25, 2005 3:49 pm Reichu wrote: Kind of funny that the top of the pyramid is where Gendo's office was.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jul 25, 2005 3:55 pm Just as well he was elsewhere when Zeruel knocked the capstone off, then.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Jul 25, 2005 3:58 pm DatDude wrote: Reichu wrote: Kind of funny that the top of the pyramid is where Gendo's office was.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Mon Jul 25, 2005 5:12 pm man this guy puts himself at the top of everything. his pyramid, the tree of life, umm stuff... lol ok maybe just those 2 and there in the same room too.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 25, 2005 5:36 pm Shin-seiki wrote: Gendo's office is a huge room with a window that runs corner-to-corner on all four sides. You can see the window near the top of the pyramid in exterior shots.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Jul 25, 2005 5:38 pm Reichu wrote: Shin-seiki wrote: Gendo's office is a huge room with a window that runs corner-to-corner on all four sides. You can see the window near the top of the pyramid in exterior shots.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jul 25, 2005 6:55 pm Reichu wrote: Kind of funny that the top of the pyramid is where Gendo's office was.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 26, 2005 12:21 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Actually, I think Gendou's office is at the top of the thin tower next to the pyramid.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Tue Jul 26, 2005 8:08 am Intresting. I bet he watches Shinji/Asuka hentai on that 15' display when he's bored.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jul 26, 2005 10:26 am So there is a back wall in the office? But why dooes the glass slope inwards?

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Tue Jul 26, 2005 10:39 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: So there is a back wall in the office?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jul 26, 2005 10:48 am What is overanalysis anyway? Here's something to chew over. In episode #19, as Mr. Tines already noted, Zeruel did blow the pyramids capstone off. Perhaps Gendou has two offices? [URL=http://imageshack.us] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Tue Jul 26, 2005 10:53 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: What is overanalysis anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jul 26, 2005 11:07 am Soluzar wrote: I would think you should be able to clearly see, as I know I can, that Gendo's office is far below the area damaged in that picture.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Tue Jul 26, 2005 12:15 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Here's something to chew over. In episode #19, as Mr. Tines already noted, Zeruel did blow the pyramids capstone off. Perhaps Gendou has two offices?

Thankfully, continuity didn't screw up on that one, so Yui-sama is pylon-less for the remainder of eternity.
